data.reasoningText:
Now I need to collect all packages and their versions, then pick the highest version for conflicts:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests | Highest |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - | 1.13.2 |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - | 8.0.1 |


| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - | 1.15.0 |
| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 | 2.9.3 |

Now I'm creating a centralized package management file with all the resolved versions, then I'll need to strip out the version attributes from each project file. Let me first check if this file already exists in the solution.

data.content:
Now I'll create `Directory.Packages.props` and update all four `.csproj` files to remove versions.

Conflict resolution (highest version wins):
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 (Api/Shared/Tests) vs 8.0.4 (Worker) → **10.0.1**
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 (Api) vs 1.11.4 (Shared) → **1.13.2**
